---
title: 'The Word of God is the Will of God.'
date: '2012-01-21T10:23:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8386620649776459856'
blogger_filename: '/2012/01/the-word-of-god-is-will-of-god.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Studies'
  - 'Will of God'
---

<br /><h1 style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: dotted; border-bottom-width: 1px; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 20px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 10px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 5px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;">The&nbsp;Word</h1><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The Word of God is the Will of God.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">A passing search of all verses that contain both the words “Word” and “God” reveal an overwhelming picture of the value God places on HIS word. If you combine that with a study of every verse that uses the words “Word” “Words” “Commands” “Law” etc… you find a definite picture developing. God takes HIS word seriously and He takes YOUR words seriously. Let’s examine the Word of God.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The worlds were framed and made through and by the Word of God. The Word of God was in the beginning, the word was with God and was God. ALL things that were created through the Word of God and nothing was created without the Word of God. The Grass may wither but the Word of God endures forever. Man shall not live on bread alone but on every word that proceeds from the mouth of God.&nbsp; The Word of God will NEVER fail! The Word of God is a seed. The Word of God is to be obeyed. The word is the will of God. The Word of God is alive and active. Sharper than any double edged sword, it penetrates even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow, it judges the thoughts and attitudes of the heart.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">We are born again of the incorruptible seed of the Word of God. The Word’s of God are the Words of the AMEN (SO BE IT)(Make it so), the faithful and true witness the ruler of God’s Creation (Jesus). The Word of God cannot be Changed, Added to, or Subtracted from. The Word of God is sweeter than Honey.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Despite all this the Word of God can be nullified (Made of no useful effect to the hearer) through traditions of man.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">God is His word… The Word of God IS God… Jesus and His Word are one.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">If this sounds odd to you then consider for a moment that you are your word as well. You can sign your name to a binding contract and it will speak for you long after you are dead. Because you agreed to those written words. You’ve heard the saying “A man’s word is his bond.” This is speaking of the closeness words have to our very being. Words are so powerful that the universe was created from them. You hear many Darwinian believers mock Christians accusing us of&nbsp; saying that the world was created from nothing. They are right to mock us if that is what we say. The bible does not say that the world was created out of nothing. It was created out of that which is not visible:</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Hebrews 11: 3</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em><sup>3</sup>&nbsp;By faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that the things which are seen were not made of things which are visible.</em></div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Words are not visible… but they are the most powerful creative force on this world or any other. Having been created in His Image and His likeness (Gen 1), man’s words are also a powerful creative force. They can be used to tear down or build up. Satan instructs Whiches and Warlocks to cast spells because he understand the power of words spoken by a believing heart. Unfortunately they are often better at using words on purpose then Christians are. We use silly saying such as “Sticks and Stones may break your bones but words can never hurt me.” Nothing could be further from the truth. You don’t have to believe the words of a fool or allow them to hurt you by any means. But they can and will hurt you if you are not aware of this principle.</div><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">If we are to understand the Will of God we must begin to take much more seriously the WORD of God. If God said it, that settles it! There is no theology or debate. If God says a matter than it is forever settled in heaven and earth. So in Order to find out what the Will of God is we must see what the Word of God says. Once God settles a matter in His word, all that is left is to believe it, and act on it.</div><h2 style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: dotted; border-bottom-width: 1px; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 20px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 10px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 5px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;">Hebrews 11:1-3&nbsp;(New King James Version)</h2><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><sup>1</sup>&nbsp;Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.&nbsp;<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;For by it the elders obtained a<em>good</em>&nbsp;testimony.<br /><sup>3</sup>&nbsp;By faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that the things which are seen were not made of things which are visible.</div><h2 id="passage_heading" style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: dotted; border-bottom-width: 1px; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 20px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 10px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 5px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;">John 1:1-4&nbsp;(New King James Version)</h2><div style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><sup>1</sup>&nbsp;In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.&nbsp;<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;He was in the beginning with God.&nbsp;<sup>3</sup>&nbsp;All things were made through Him, and without Him nothing was made that was made.&nbsp;<sup>4</sup>&nbsp;In Him was life, and the life was the light of men.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+40:8&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Isaiah 40:8</a></strong><br />The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of our&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;endures forever.”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+18:30&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Psalm 18:30</a></strong><br />As for&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>, his way is perfect: The LORD’s&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;is flawless; he shields all who take refuge in him.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+56:4&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Psalm 56:4</a></strong><br />In&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>, whose&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;I praise— in&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;I trust and am not afraid. What can mere mortals do to me?</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Proverbs+30:5&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Proverbs 30:5</a></strong><br />“Every&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;is flawless; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+2:3&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Isaiah 2:3</a></strong><br />Many peoples will come and say, “Come, let us go up to the mountain of the LORD, to the temple of the&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;of Jacob. He will teach us his ways, so that we may walk in his paths.” The law will go out from Zion, the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of the LORD from Jerusalem.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+8:20&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Isaiah 8:20</a></strong><br />Consult&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>’s instruction and the testimony of warning. If anyone does not speak according to this&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>, they have no light of dawn.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+40:8&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Isaiah 40:8</a></strong><br />The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of our&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;endures forever.”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Jeremiah+15:16&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Jeremiah 15:16</a></strong><br />When your&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s came, I ate them; they were my joy and my heart’s delight, for I bear your name, LORD&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>Almighty.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Micah+4:2&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Micah 4:2</a></strong><br />Many nations will come and say, “Come, let us go up to the mountain of the LORD, to the temple of the&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;of Jacob. He will teach us his ways, so that we may walk in his paths.” The law will go out from Zion, the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of the LORD from Jerusalem.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+4:4&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 4:4</a></strong><br />Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;that comes from the mouth of<strong>God</strong>.’ ”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+15:6&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 15:6</a></strong><br />they are not to ‘honor their father or mother’ with it. Thus you nullify the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;for the sake of your tradition.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+7:13&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Mark 7:13</a></strong><br />Thus you nullify the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that.”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+1:37&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Luke 1:37</a></strong><br />For no&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;from&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;will ever fail.”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+8:11&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Luke 8:11</a></strong><br />“This is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+11:28&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Luke 11:28</a></strong><br />He replied, “Blessed rather are those who hear the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;and obey it.”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10:35&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">John 10:35</a></strong><br />If he called them ‘<strong>god</strong>s,’ to whom the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;came—and Scripture cannot be set aside—</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+20:32&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Acts 20:32</a></strong><br />“Now I commit you to&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;and to the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of his grace, which can build you up and give you an inheritance among all those who are sanctified.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+22:14&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Acts 22:14</a></strong><br />“Then he said: ‘The&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will and to see the Righteous One and to hear&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s from his mouth.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ephesians+6:17&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Ephesians 6:17</a></strong><br />Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20Thessalonians+2:13&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">1 Thessalonians 2:13</a></strong><br />And we also thank&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;continually because, when you received the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>, which you heard from us, you accepted it not as a human&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>, but as it actually is, the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>, which is indeed at work in you who believe.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20Timothy+4:5&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">1 Timothy 4:5</a></strong><br />because it is consecrated by the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;and prayer.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2%20Timothy+2:15&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">2 Timothy 2:15</a></strong><br />Do your best to present yourself to&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;as one approved, a worker who does not need to be ashamed and who correctly handles the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of truth.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+1:3&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Hebrews 1:3</a></strong><br />The Son is the radiance of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>’s glory and the exact representation of his being, sustaining all things by his powerful&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+4:12&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Hebrews 4:12</a></strong><br />For the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;is alive and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it penetrates even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow; it judges the thoughts and attitudes of the heart.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+5:12&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Hebrews 5:12</a></strong><br />In fact, though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you the elementary truths of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>’s<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;all over again. You need milk, not solid food!</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+6:5&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Hebrews 6:5</a></strong><br />who have tasted the goodness of the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;and the powers of the coming age</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+13:7&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Hebrews 13:7</a></strong><br />Remember your leaders, who spoke the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;to you. Consider the outcome of their way of life and imitate their faith.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20Peter+1:23&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">1 Peter 1:23</a></strong><br />For you have been born again, not of perishable seed, but of imperishable, through the living and enduring&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of<strong>God</strong>.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2%20Peter+3:5&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">2 Peter 3:5</a></strong><br />But they deliberately forget that long ago by&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>’s&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;the heavens came into being and the earth was formed out of water and by water.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20John+2:5&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">1 John 2:5</a></strong><br />But if anyone obeys his&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>, love for&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;is truly made complete in them. This is how we know we are in him:</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20John+2:14&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">1 John 2:14</a></strong><br />I write to you, dear children, because you know the Father. I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you are strong, and the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;lives in you, and you have overcome the evil one</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1:2&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 1:2</a></strong><br />who testifies to everything he saw—that is, the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;and the testimony of Jesus Christ.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+2:18&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 2:18</a></strong><br />[&nbsp;<em>To the Church in Thyatira</em>&nbsp;] “To the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These are the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s of the Son of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>, whose eyes are like blazing fire and whose feet are like burnished bronze.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+3:14&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 3:14</a></strong><br />[&nbsp;<em>To the Church in Laodicea</em>&nbsp;] “To the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These are the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>’s creation.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+19:13&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 19:13</a></strong><br />He is dressed in a robe dipped in blood, and his name is the&nbsp;<strong>Word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+20:4&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 20:4</a></strong><br />I saw thrones on which were seated those who had been given authority to judge. And I saw the souls of those who had been beheaded because of their testimony about Jesus and because of the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>God</strong></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+22:9&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 22:9</a></strong><br />But he said to me, “Don’t do that! I am a fellow servant with you and with your fellow prophets and with all who keep the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s of this scroll. Worship&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>!”</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+22:18&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 22:18</a></strong><br />I warn everyone who hears the&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s of the prophecy of this scroll: If anyone adds anything to them,&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;will add to that person the plagues described in this scroll.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+22:19&amp;version=NIV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Revelation 22:19</a></strong><br />And if anyone takes&nbsp;<strong>word</strong>s away from this scroll of prophecy,&nbsp;<strong>God</strong>&nbsp;will take away from that person any share in the tree of life and in the Holy City, which are described in this scroll.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+119:103&amp;version=NKJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Psalm 119:103</a></strong><br />How sweet are Your&nbsp;<strong>words</strong>&nbsp;to my taste,Sweeter than&nbsp;<strong>honey</strong>&nbsp;to my mouth!</div>
